requirements.requirement: ERM Admin: maintain governance hierarchy, categories, rating scales, appetite levels
requirements.whatExists: Entity hierarchy (group/subsidiary/department kinds), 6 seeded risk categories, rating bands via bandForScore(), 5-tier appetite levels with propose-approve workflow.
requirements.whatsMissing: Categories, rating scales/bands and hierarchy are hardcoded constants; no admin CRUD UI at runtime; no department matrix UI.
requirements.notes: Data model is sound; lift is an admin config surface plus moving constants to editable records. Moderate effort, no external dependency.

requirements.requirement: ERM Admin: create & manage users and assignments (Champions, Owners, Control Owners)
requirements.whatsMissing: No user-management module; personas are a fixed 4-role enum in localStorage; owners are free-text. No UI to create users or assign roles.
requirements.notes: Foundational gap. Needs identity store, persistence and SSO wiring. Prerequisite for Risk Owner/Control Owner roles and real RBAC.

requirements.requirement: ERM Admin: create/maintain risks with full detail (inherent/residual L&I, calculated ratings, controls linkage)
requirements.whatExists: Full cause-event-impact structure, dual scoring, auto-banded inherent/residual ratings, control linkage for seeded and user-drafted risks.
requirements.whatsMissing: Nothing material.
requirements.notes: Strongest item in the group; core risk record is demo-ready and complete.

requirements.requirement: ERM Admin: review, update & approve risk records across all functions/BUs
requirements.whatExists: Cross-entity review/update and CRUD with audit logging and Group-level visibility; drafts carry open/in_progress/closed status.
requirements.whatsMissing: No formal approval gate (no pending sign-off → approved before merge to register).
requirements.notes: Visibility exists; the approval workflow is the build. Pairs with the sign-off engine in item 1.10.

requirements.requirement: ERM Admin: create, assign & track mitigation actions and treatment plans (by person/function)
requirements.whatExists: Per-risk mitigation actions with owner, due date, status workflow and live overdue computation, persisted and audited.
requirements.whatsMissing: Assignment is free-text owner (no structured person/function binding); treatment library not assignable via UI.
requirements.notes: Largely there; gap closes once user records (1.2) exist to bind assignments to.

requirements.requirement: ERM Admin: monitor overdue reviews, overdue actions, ensure periodic risk updates
requirements.whatExists: Overdue mitigation actions computed and surfaced live; an ERM annual plan tracks portfolio-wide activity status.
requirements.whatsMissing: No per-risk nextReviewDate/reviewFrequency, so overdue-review monitoring and periodic-update enforcement are absent.
requirements.notes: Overdue-actions half is done; review-cycle scheduling needs a small data-model addition plus surfacing.

requirements.requirement: Risk Champion (department): create/maintain risks with same full detail structure
requirements.whatExists: Risk Champion persona exists; user drafts carry identical full CEI/dual-scoring detail with validation and entity scoping.
requirements.whatsMissing: Department binding is by persona scope only; RBAC is demo-mode (can() returns true when persona null) so department enforcement is inactive.
requirements.notes: Functionally complete; gap is enforcement, resolved by activating RBAC (1.12), not new risk-form work.

requirements.requirement: Risk Champion: update mitigation plans/status/evidence; convert completed mitigation into a control
requirements.whatExists: Mitigation status/progress/description updates supported; a separate control model with owner/frequency exists.
requirements.whatsMissing: No completion-evidence upload and no promote-mitigation-to-control conversion flow bridging the two models.
requirements.notes: Two discrete builds: evidence capture and the mitigation→control bridge. Evidence upload depends on shared file-storage (1.11).

requirements.requirement: Risk Champion: add & maintain KRIs (frequency Q/SA/A, thresholds vs appetite, commentary, status)
requirements.whatExists: 16 seeded KRIs, risk-linked, with editable amber/red thresholds, value entry, deterministic traffic-light status and appetite linkage.
requirements.whatsMissing: Champions cannot add KRIs at runtime; no semi-annual frequency; no per-KRI approval/appetite-enforcement gate.
requirements.notes: Monitoring logic is solid; remaining work is an add-KRI UI and a frequency/approval extension. Self-contained.

requirements.requirement: Risk Owner: own & accountable; create risks OR assign Champion; sign-off assessments/plans/updates
requirements.whatsMissing: No Risk Owner persona; owner is free-text; no owner→champion delegation; no formal sign-off/attestation workflow.
requirements.notes: Accountability backbone. Depends on user management (1.2) and approval gate (1.4); attestation engine is net-new. High priority.

requirements.requirement: Control Owner: own controls, confirm status & effectiveness, support periodic review, provide evidence
requirements.whatExists: Controls carry owner, status (effective/partial/failed), effectiveness scores, last-tested/next-due dates and a deterministic test engine.
requirements.whatsMissing: No Control Owner persona, no owner-driven status-confirmation workflow; evidence is narrative-only (no file upload/versioning).
requirements.notes: Control data model is mature; gaps are the persona (1.2 dependency) and evidence storage (shared with 1.8).

requirements.requirement: Role-based permissions / RBAC enforcing the accountability model
requirements.whatExists: 4-persona model mapped to IIA 3-Lines with 24 granular permissions, can(), canAccessRoute() and route allowlists.
requirements.whatsMissing: Not enforced: demo-mode bypass returns true when persona null; no middleware route gating; no Risk Owner/Control Owner roles; SSO unwired.
requirements.notes: Well-architected but inert. Enforcement plus 5+ role expansion is the linchpin for 1.2, 1.7, 1.10, 1.11.

requirements.requirement: Standardized risk record: statement, scope (group/segment/department), category, strategic objective
requirements.whatExists: Standardized statement, ID, risk category (6 hardcoded values), multi-entity group/segment scoping.
requirements.whatsMissing: Department scope is free-text only; no explicit link from a risk to a strategic objective.
requirements.notes: Low effort: add a structured department picker and a strategic-objective FK. Objective linkage needs a maintained objective master list.

requirements.requirement: Root causes, impacts/consequences and risk drivers captured
requirements.whatExists: Mandatory Cause-Event-Impact triple plus up to 16 weighted KRI/operational drivers propagating into scores, shown in the detail drawer.
requirements.notes: Fully met by both lenses. A genuine strength; demo-ready as-is.

requirements.requirement: Inherent assessment (likelihood/impact) with automatic inherent rating
requirements.whatExists: Inherent = baseLikelihood x baseImpact on a 1-5 scale (max 25), with auto-mapping to Low/Medium/High/Critical.
requirements.notes: Fully met. Automatic banding implemented and consistent with residual logic.

requirements.requirement: Control environment summary (existing controls and their owners) on the risk
requirements.whatExists: Risks carry typed controls with composite effectiveness; a separate 20-control ICOFR library holds owners and test status.
requirements.whatsMissing: Per-control owner not wired into register-level control object or surfaced as a risk-level summary; linkage is effectively one-to-one.
requirements.notes: Moderate effort: extend the control object with an owner field and a one-to-many risk-to-control join. ICOFR owner data exists to draw from.

requirements.requirement: Residual assessment with automatic residual rating
requirements.whatExists: Residual = inherent x (1 − compositeEffectiveness), auto-calculated and auto-rated through the same banding function as inherent.
requirements.notes: Fully met. Calculation chain is sound and ties cleanly to the control effectiveness model.

requirements.requirement: Capture risk movement (increased/decreased/stable) with reasons & dates → period-over-period trend reporting
requirements.whatsMissing: Scores, exposure and ratings are point-in-time only. No historical snapshot table, movement flag, reason/date capture, or trend view.
requirements.notes: Highest-effort item in the group: requires a time-series store and snapshotting architecture. Foundational for trend reporting.

requirements.requirement: Each risk has a defined risk owner and assigned department
requirements.whatExists: Every risk has an assigned owner.
requirements.whatsMissing: Owner is free-text with no RBAC binding; no structured department/BU assignment or delegation chain.
requirements.notes: Depends on the same department hierarchy and user/role model needed for 2.1. Bundle owner-role and department work together.

requirements.requirement: Periodic review cycles: last review date, next review due, overdue status & escalation
requirements.whatExists: A portfolio-wide ERM annual plan exists, but it covers programme activities, not individual risk attestation.
requirements.whatsMissing: No per-risk lastReview/nextReviewDate/reviewFrequency/approvalStatus fields, no overdue logic, no review-driven escalation.
requirements.notes: Build end-to-end: review-cycle fields, overdue computation, escalation workflow. Pairs with 2.6's date/history work.

requirements.requirement: Controls record: description, owner, frequency, risk linkage, status, effectiveness
requirements.whatExists: 20 ICOFR-aligned controls carry description, owner, frequency, linked risk ID, status (effective/partial/failed) and a deterministic effectiveness score.
requirements.whatsMissing: Status is effective/partial/failed rather than a literal active/inactive lifecycle toggle — a labelling nuance, not a functional gap.
requirements.notes: Fully demonstrable today; if the client insists on an explicit active/inactive flag it is a trivial field add.

requirements.requirement: Controls reusable across multiple risks (no duplication)
requirements.whatsMissing: Each control hard-links to exactly one risk ID; no many-to-many model, so a shared control must be duplicated per risk.
requirements.notes: Needs a control-to-risk junction table plus reverse-coverage UI; production-lean schema change, low risk.

requirements.requirement: Treatment plans/actions linked to risks: description, owner, due date, progress status, completion evidence/notes
requirements.whatExists: Per-risk mitigation actions persist id, owner, due date, status (open/in_progress/closed) and free-text description.
requirements.whatsMissing: No structured completion-evidence or notes capture and no document upload to close the loop.
requirements.notes: Core record exists; adding an evidence/notes field plus attachment closes the audit loop — modest effort.

requirements.requirement: Assign actions to individuals or departments/functions
requirements.whatExists: Actions carry a free-text owner (individual name or role).
requirements.whatsMissing: No structured department/function entity or RBAC-linked assignment; departmental routing is nominal text only.
requirements.notes: Depends on a department hierarchy and owner-to-role model; coordinate with planned RBAC work to avoid rework.

requirements.requirement: Clear visibility of overdue actions and action bottlenecks
requirements.whatExists: Overdue computed live and surfaced in register column, detail drawer and dashboard open-mitigations grid.
requirements.whatsMissing: No bottleneck/ageing analytics — ageing buckets, stalled in_progress actions, owner workload concentration.
requirements.notes: Overdue visibility already convincing in a demo; bottleneck analytics is an aggregation/reporting layer added in MVP.

requirements.requirement: Treatment-to-control transition: on completion/acceptance, record outcome as a new/enhanced control with history
requirements.whatExists: Only the reverse bridge exists — a failed control auto-generates a mitigation action.
requirements.whatsMissing: No path to promote a completed/accepted mitigation into a new or enhanced control with version history.
requirements.notes: Highest-effort item: needs a closed-loop lifecycle across actions and controls libraries plus an immutable history store.

requirements.requirement: Define KRIs linked to risks: name, frequency, thresholds vs appetite, status visualization, commentary
requirements.whatExists: 8 KRIs fully defined with IDs, names, frequencies, linkedRiskIds, editable amber/red thresholds anchored to an appetite statement/approver, traffic-light status with 6-month sparkline, breach history, per-entry commentary.
requirements.whatsMissing: Thresholds manually reconciled to appetite (no auto-validation); frequency supports only monthly/quarterly not Q/SA/A.
requirements.notes: Strongest of the three — demo-ready today. Only polish: add SA/Annual cadence and auto-check thresholds against the appetite figure.

requirements.requirement: KRIs assigned to an accountable owner responsible for timely updates & breach explanations
requirements.whatExists: Every KRI carries a named owner field; value entries record enteredBy attribution; breach history captures status transitions with commentary.
requirements.whatsMissing: Owner is static free-text — no RBAC binding, delegation tracking, update-due reminder/SLA, or enforced breach-explanation gate.
requirements.notes: Accountability is displayed, not enforced. Wiring owner into RBAC plus a breach-commentary gate and update-due SLA is production-lean.

requirements.requirement: Support KRI values sourced from other business systems / authoritative data sources
requirements.whatsMissing: All KRI values are manual form entries or illustrative seed data; no live ingestion, API connectors, scheduling, or real-time monitoring. Integration marked pending.
requirements.notes: Requires dedicated data-pipeline/connector architecture and depends on client granting source-system access — scope as a separate integration workstream.

requirements.requirement: Risk dashboards at group/segment/department: by category & rating, top risks & movement, inherent vs residual distribution
requirements.whatExists: Group and segment dashboards with 5x5 heatmaps, rating-count chips, top-10 risk tables.
requirements.whatsMissing: Binning by strategic category and department; top-risk movement-over-time; side-by-side inherent-vs-residual. Department slicing blocked (no department dimension).
requirements.notes: Category binning and inherent-vs-residual charts are quick wins on existing data; department slicing needs a department field added first.

requirements.requirement: Appetite & tolerance view: position vs group appetite/tolerance, configurable to framework with escalation triggers
requirements.whatExists: Single global exposure-ceiling and score-ceiling position vs appetite with over-appetite warnings, plus a propose/approve appetite-statement override workflow.
requirements.whatsMissing: Per-category and per-portfolio tolerance bands; framework-configurable tiers; per-risk appetite ceilings; automated breach-to-escalation trigger pipeline.
requirements.notes: Heaviest lift in the group; configurable tolerance tiers plus automated escalation is a true build. Sequence after data-model work in 5.1.

requirements.requirement: Controls & mitigation dashboards: controls by status/effectiveness, action progress, overdue by owner/function, treatment impact on residual
requirements.whatExists: Controls by status/effectiveness, action progress with live overdue, treatment-impact-on-residual via residual = inherent x (1 − composite effectiveness).
requirements.whatsMissing: A consolidated view slicing overdue items by owner/function. Residual-math is built but needs a dedicated treatment-impact visualization.
requirements.notes: Closest to complete in the group. Overdue-by-owner/function is a straightforward grouping over existing data; low effort, high demo value.

requirements.requirement: KRI dashboards: within vs breached, breaches by category/department, recurring breaches & trend over time
requirements.whatExists: Traffic-light within-vs-breached status, deterministic breach history with status transitions, 6-month sparkline trends.
requirements.whatsMissing: Breach aggregation by category/department (blocked: no department dimension); recurring-breach detection and breach-frequency-over-time roll-up.
requirements.notes: Recurring-breach analytics buildable on the existing event log; category/department aggregation depends on the same department-dimension work as 5.1.

requirements.requirement: Standardized outputs for departments, segments, group top management
requirements.whatExists: Segment and group tiers strong: Portfolio Control Tower with per-subsidiary heatmaps, ARC Pack, Executive Brief, top-10 risks, appetite-breach warnings, 12-month KRI trends.
requirements.whatsMissing: Department-tier deliverables. No department hierarchy/BU matrix, so no department-scoped register or standalone action-tracker report.
requirements.notes: Two of three tiers already demo-ready; gap is the structured department dimension. Sequence after owner/department data model is hardened.

requirements.requirement: Reports include narrative-ready insights (what changed & why, breaches & required actions, top overdue actions)
requirements.whatExists: AI Executive Brief/ARC narratives surface breaches, required actions, strategic implications; register-critic flags breaches; overdue mitigations computed live.
requirements.whatsMissing: 'What changed & why' unsupported. Platform is point-in-time with no historical snapshots or top-risk movement tracking to ground deltas.
requirements.notes: Narrative engine is the strong asset; missing piece is the historical snapshot store — same dependency as 6.3, build once.

requirements.requirement: Periodic reporting cycle support: consistent cut-off dates, data freeze/versioning, sign-offs
requirements.whatExists: Annual-plan calendar only. ARC Pack sign-off block exists as a structural template.
requirements.whatsMissing: No data freeze/cut-off, no report/scenario versioning, no historical snapshot table, no scheduled pack generation, no executable e-sign-off with audit-recorded approval.
requirements.notes: True greenfield governance build requiring a server-side snapshot store and sign-off workflow. Prerequisite for 6.2's delta tracking.

requirements.requirement: Formal review/approval workflow (risk creation, rating changes & closures, mitigation closure & conversion to controls)
requirements.whatExists: A role-gated workflow engine supports multi-step approval chains for risk_approval, mitigation_closure, appetite_change and kri_threshold_change, with seeded in-flight instances. Appetite propose-approve runs end-to-end.
requirements.whatsMissing: Workflows are demo-seeded, not enforced as a mandatory gate before a draft merges. No workflow kinds for rating-change or convert-mitigation-to-control. No maker-checker enforcement or persisted sign-off.
requirements.notes: Engine de-risks this: the hard part is built. Effort is wiring it as a blocking gate plus two new workflow kinds and persistence.

requirements.requirement: Traceability/history (who changed ratings/details & when, mitigation update/closure history, KRI historical values & breach history)
requirements.whatExists: AuditTrailContext logs actor + action + timestamp for all risk/mitigation CRUD; workflow instances carry per-transition history; KRI values and breach history tracked.
requirements.whatsMissing: Storage is client-side volatile localStorage with free-text diffs. No tamper-proof append-only server store, no field-level before/after capture, no point-in-time rating history.
requirements.notes: Demo-credible today but not audit-grade. Production gap is a server-side immutable store with structured diffs — standard backend work.

requirements.requirement: Multi-level org (Group → Segment → Department) with consistent roll-up and consolidated group view
requirements.whatExists: hierarchy.ts models a holding plus 4 subsidiaries (segments); Portfolio Control Tower does Group↔subsidiary heatmap roll-up, consolidation and escalation.
requirements.whatsMissing: Department tier is declared but has zero instances. Owner is free-text with no department binding, RBAC delegation, or department-level roll-up.
requirements.notes: Top two of three levels are real and demo-strong. Department spine plus ownership routing is the remaining slice — modest model work.

requirements.requirement: Emerging risks: flag & track separately
requirements.whatExists: Register Critic AI detects 3-5 emerging/missing risks from external signals on demand and surfaces them as suggestions.
requirements.whatsMissing: No structured emerging-risk flag/status on the risk model, no dedicated watchlist/register, no lifecycle or promotion-to-register path.
requirements.notes: AI surfacing is the differentiator and it works. Adding a first-class flag, watchlist and promote-path is light MVP work — good early win.

requirements.requirement: Issues/incidents linkage to risks
requirements.whatsMissing: No incident/issue entity in the type model; 'incident' appears only in narrative text. No object with status/owner linked back to risks or failed controls.
requirements.notes: Net-new entity but cleanly reuses existing action/audit patterns and the failed-control auto-action hook. Low conceptual risk.

requirements.requirement: Formal risk acceptance decisions for residual above appetite (rationale + approving authority)
requirements.whatExists: Dashboards compute and warn on 'over appetite'; three-lines narrative references ARC/board sanctioning acceptances.
requirements.whatsMissing: No per-risk appetite ceiling, no breach-to-acceptance workflow, no captured acceptance record (rationale, approver, expiry).
requirements.notes: Highest-value governance gap for an above-appetite posture. Reuses the existing appetite-change approval workflow, so production-lean.

requirements.requirement: Lessons learned capture post-treatment/post-event
requirements.whatsMissing: No lessons-learned, post-mortem or post-event capture anywhere in the data model or UI.
requirements.notes: Straightforward new record type (author, date, narrative) linked to a risk/incident/mitigation closure, plugging into the existing audit trail. Lowest priority of the group.

requirements.requirement: External risk signal monitoring via web-search/news APIs + RSS (financial/regulatory/cyber/macro) on a schedule
requirements.whatExists: 8+ news/macro sources ingested (Google News RSS, GDELT, Guardian, NewsAPI, Reuters, World Bank, IMF, FT) with 5-min caching and mock fallback.
requirements.whatsMissing: No scheduled/cron polling (on-demand only); no UAE regulatory-portal feeds (CBUAE/SCA/ADGM) or cyber-advisory feeds (CISA/NCA).
requirements.notes: Breadth of macro/news feeds is strong; gaps are a scheduler job plus 4-5 regional/cyber connectors — lean MVP work, no architectural risk.

requirements.requirement: AI correlates signals to register risks → flagged summary mapped to affected risks for review (no auto-updates)
requirements.whatExists: ai-classify (Claude) maps headlines to portfolio/BU impact, severity and confidence; fusion endpoint cross-correlates external to internal signals; all outputs advisory with no auto-write.
requirements.whatsMissing: Nothing material — the approval-gated mapping is fully in place.
requirements.notes: Fully met. Demonstrable today and directly satisfies the no-auto-update governance intent — a confident headline item.

requirements.requirement: AI emerging-risk suggestions: propose new risk entries (draft statement, category, suggested owner) to accept/dismiss
requirements.whatExists: Register Critic surfaces 3-5 missing risks with recommendations; Documents workflow extracts draft risks with category and an explicit accept/dismiss approval loop.
requirements.whatsMissing: Suggested owner is inferred in narrative form rather than a structured, selectable field.
requirements.notes: Effectively met. Only polish is promoting suggested-owner to a structured field — minor, do not gate the demo on it.

requirements.requirement: AI in-context risk-record recommendations: root causes/impacts, benchmark controls (ISO/COSO/NIST), mitigations, KRIs & thresholds — advisory
requirements.whatExists: Deterministic AI Suggestions provide per-category mitigation playbooks and appetite-anchored KRI threshold recommendations; control-assessment-ai critiques existing controls.
requirements.whatsMissing: Recommendations not surfaced inline on the individual risk record; no generation of additional root causes/impacts; no benchmark mapping to ISO 31000/COSO/NIST.
requirements.notes: Engine exists but isn't wired into the risk drawer and lacks framework benchmarking. Value is in surfacing it in-context plus a control-framework reference set.

requirements.requirement: What-if scenario simulation: named scenario, adjust L/I for selected risks, recalc ratings, side-by-side vs current profile
requirements.whatExists: Real-time workbench with 8-15 driver sliders and intensity multipliers; live per-portfolio impact/exposure recalculation; baseline-vs-simulation panels and named scenario templates.
requirements.whatsMissing: Adjustment is driver-based, not direct per-risk L/I override; comparison is baseline-vs-scenario rather than dedicated current-profile side-by-side; scenarios not user-named on the fly.
requirements.notes: Strong workbench already; the literal per-risk L/I editing is the delta. MVP to add per-risk overrides and a true profile comparison view.

requirements.requirement: Scenario outputs: appetite breaches under scenario, change in rating counts (L/M/H/Critical), AI narrative
requirements.whatExists: Outputs include per-portfolio AED/% impact, AI mitigation/opportunity narrative and a tiered cost-of-inaction response plan.
requirements.whatsMissing: No explicit appetite-breach list under scenario; no before/after delta of L/M/H/Critical rating counts.
requirements.notes: Narrative and financial outputs are rich; the two missing computed views are straightforward roll-ups over data already present — MVP-grade.

requirements.requirement: Scenarios saved & versioned; compare scenarios vs each other or baseline
requirements.whatsMissing: Scenarios are ephemeral, computed in-memory; no save/load persistence, no versioning/rollback, no Scenario A vs B comparison.
requirements.notes: Explicitly deferred post-MVP. Requires a persistence layer, comparison engine and decision log — scope as a dedicated build phase.

requirements.section: P3-P5. AI Control Intelligence, Copilot & Narratives
requirements.requirement: AI control gap & duplication detection: duplicate controls, high-rated risks with no/ineffective controls, controls with no owner or overdue review
requirements.whatExists: controlTestingEngine deterministically flags overdue/due-soon and fail/partial controls; owner is a structured field; register-critic and control-assessment-ai flag weak and missing controls.
requirements.whatsMissing: No near-duplicate CONTROL detection (Jaccard dedup is risk-only) and no packaged 'high-rated risk with no effective control' or 'overdue effectiveness review' gap detector surfaced to the user.
requirements.notes: Detectors are production-lean work on existing data/fields, not net-new architecture; reuse the risk Jaccard logic for controls. Moderate effort, high visibility.

requirements.section: P3-P5. AI Control Intelligence, Copilot & Narratives
requirements.requirement: AI control benchmarking: suggest alignment to ISO 31000/COSO/NIST with a framework reference tag
requirements.whatsMissing: Controls carry ICOFR assertion tags only; no ISO 31000/COSO/NIST mapping or framework-reference field anywhere in the code.
requirements.notes: Lowest-effort item: a single Claude prompt emitting a framework tag per control/gap is a quick API-only POC. Quick win for the demo.

requirements.section: P3-P5. AI Control Intelligence, Copilot & Narratives
requirements.requirement: AI Copilot chat (all roles), read-only context of register/controls/KRI/action tracker; NL queries; drafts only
requirements.whatExists: risk-chat / Risk Memory Chat: persona-available, read-only, grounded Q&A over register, KRIs, controls, appetite, mitigations and escalations with NL + exact-ID lookups, a 'never invent a record' guardrail, text-only replies.
requirements.whatsMissing: Full action-tracker context and per-role scoping are thin — overdue counts surfaced rather than the full action list.
requirements.notes: Strongest item in the group; satisfies read-only + drafts-only intent today. Only polish: widen action-tracker grounding and tighten role scoping.

requirements.section: P3-P5. AI Control Intelligence, Copilot & Narratives
requirements.requirement: AI-generated reporting narratives: top risk movements & drivers, appetite breaches & response, overdue action summary, emerging signals — editable, not auto-published
requirements.whatExists: Executive Brief and ARC Pack generate AI narratives on drivers, top risks, recommended actions and emerging signals, tagged as human-approval 'hypothesis' (not auto-published).
requirements.whatsMissing: Risk movement-over-time and appetite-breach/overdue-action narratives are weak (point-in-time data); briefs are regenerate-only with no in-place edit/annotate/version-before-send workflow.
requirements.notes: Two real dependencies: a historical snapshot store for true movement/variance, and an in-UI edit/version workflow. The not-auto-published guardrail is already in place.

requirements.requirement: Capital Projects risk module: cost overrun, schedule delay, contractor, procurement, HSE; MS Project/Primavera P6 integration; AI flags deteriorating SPI/CPI
requirements.whatExists: Project/Construction is a first-class risk category with seeded construction risks, cost-inflation and schedule-delay drivers in the scenario engine, and financial exposure anchored to active-project GDV/capex.
requirements.whatsMissing: No dedicated Capital Projects module, no project-controls schema (SPI/CPI earned-value, contractor scorecards, HSE incident log), no MS Project/Primavera P6 connector, no AI schedule-deterioration flagging.
requirements.notes: Highest-effort item in the group: P6/MS Project integration and earned-value data model are net-new engineering with an external-system dependency.

requirements.requirement: Incident & audit integration: log incidents & link to risks; upload audit reports → AI extracts findings/actions & proposes linkages; loss-data log
requirements.whatExists: analyze-document extracts risks/compliance issues from documents; the control-failure to action bridge plus audit-trail provide a foundation.
requirements.whatsMissing: No incident object, no loss-data register, no incident-to-risk linkage with rating/plan re-prompt loop, no audit-report-specific findings extractor or auto-linkage.
requirements.notes: Net-new incident and loss-data model plus an audit-finding pipeline layered on the existing document AI. Good early win for the incident-to-risk feedback loop.

requirements.requirement: Regulatory compliance tracking: obligation register linked to risks/controls/policies; AI monitors regulatory feeds → impact-assessment draft
requirements.whatExists: AI flags UAE regulatory signals (ADX/RERA/ADEC/CBUAE) via document intelligence and news classification; risks carry free-text source and narrative regulatory/ESG mentions.
requirements.whatsMissing: No structured obligation register linking obligations to controls/policies/departments, and no scheduled regulatory-feed horizon-scanning producing impact-assessment drafts.
requirements.notes: Substantial dedicated build: obligation-to-control-to-policy schema plus scheduled regulator feed monitoring. High client value given named regulators.

requirements.requirement: Document Intelligence: upload PDF/Word/PPT/Excel → AI parses & extracts risks/controls/actions/findings/obligations → proposed register updates
requirements.whatExists: analyze-document and control-assessment-ai parse documents, extract risks/controls/compliance issues, and surface proposed register updates through a human-approval workflow on the Documents page.
requirements.whatsMissing: Input is manual paste with no real PDF/Word/PPT/Excel file ingestion; no bulk/multi-document handling; findings/obligations extraction and the broader taxonomy are absent.
requirements.notes: Closest to production: the approval workflow and extraction skeleton exist. Core work is wiring real file upload/parsing. Best first deliverable — also unblocks V2.2 and V2.3.

additional.whatItDoes: AI reads live external signals and generates a ranked, ABC-specific priority-action list with owner, due-date, impact and confidence; rich on-click board-grade analysis (portfolio impact, act-vs-ignore consequence, source link).
additional.showcase: The headline AI USP — turns external news into prioritised executive actions.

additional.whatItDoes: Cross-correlates external market headlines with internal operational/ERP data to flag risk amplification or mitigation, with an amplification factor.
additional.showcase: Connects outside-world signals to inside-the-business data.

additional.whatItDoes: Shows how an activated scenario cascades across ABC's five business units, with a severity-tagged propagation chain and urgency signal.
additional.showcase: Demonstrates systemic/contagion thinking, not siloed risk.

additional.whatItDoes: Answers a free-form executive question against live risk context, returning key points, action items, portfolio focus and a confidence score.
additional.showcase: A second AI surface beyond the register Copilot.

additional.whatItDoes: Generates a 2-3 sentence business-impact read per portfolio with revenue-at-risk, management actions and watch indicators.
additional.showcase: Per-portfolio executive colour on demand.

additional.whatItDoes: AI critiques the live register for missing risks (e.g. climate transition, cyber), weak controls and coverage gaps.
additional.showcase: Shows the AI challenging the register, not just reading it.

additional.whatItDoes: One-click AI board brief synthesising risks, exposures, cross-portfolio propagation, strategic implications and recommendations with source traceability.
additional.showcase: Board-pack narrative in seconds.

additional.whatItDoes: Converts the executive brief into a structured Gamma.app prompt (copied to clipboard) to auto-generate a board presentation.
additional.showcase: Brief to slide-deck pipeline.

additional.whatItDoes: Quantifies scenarios in AED (Year-1 / Year-2 / Total), recovery time and mitigation strategies at mild/moderate/severe intensity.
additional.showcase: Turns likelihood x impact into money — what the board cares about.

additional.whatItDoes: Calculates and displays the financial loss of NOT acting on a mitigation if the scenario materialises.
additional.showcase: Powerful 'do nothing = AED X' framing.

additional.whatItDoes: Reusable overlay showing the cost math behind a KPI card (drill from a headline number to its calculation).
additional.showcase: Transparency — every big number is explainable.

additional.whatItDoes: Reconciles the AED 2.35Bn gross exposure into segment splits and gross to hedged to net, explaining the headline-vs-residual difference.
additional.showcase: Pre-empts the 'why two numbers?' board question.

additional.feature: Control to Risk to Financial-exposure drill-down
additional.whatItDoes: One click from a failed control to its linked risk to the AED financial impact.
additional.showcase: End-to-end traceability in a single gesture.

additional.whatItDoes: Dedicated page mapping the IIA 2020 Three Lines model to ABC roles and the platform surfaces that serve each line.
additional.showcase: Speaks the assurance language of an ARC/board.

additional.whatItDoes: Maps every ISO 31000:2018 principle and COSO ERM 2017 component to the platform surface implementing it, with live/partial/roadmap chips.
additional.showcase: Credibility: 'we are framework-aligned.'

additional.whatItDoes: Reference map of federal/market/emirate/sector regulators (CBUAE, ADX, SCA, RERA...) with ABC obligations and reporting cadence.
additional.showcase: Foundation for the V2.3 obligation register.

additional.whatItDoes: UAE sector risk library with 12+ peer-disclosed scenarios and a benchmark grid comparing ABC to Emaar/DAMAC/Sobha across ERM categories.
additional.showcase: 'How do we compare to peers?' — a board favourite.

additional.whatItDoes: Register of governance/financial/operational/HSE/IT/compliance policies with owners, review dates and linked risks.
additional.showcase: Foundation for policy-to-risk linkage.

additional.whatItDoes: Placeholder for Business Continuity (BIA/RTO/RPO, continuity plans, crisis roster, recovery strategies).
additional.showcase: Signals the BCM expansion path.

additional.whatItDoes: Board-ready Audit & Risk Committee pack with cover, posture, KRI summary, scenarios and sign-off blocks, exportable to PDF.
additional.showcase: A tangible, printable governance artefact.

additional.whatItDoes: Group to subsidiary 5x5 heatmaps with concentration analysis, escalations and an ERM scorecard.
additional.showcase: Group-CEO consolidated cockpit.

additional.feature: ERM Annual Plan calendar (plan vs actual)
additional.whatItDoes: Jan to Dec Gantt of ERM activities showing planned window vs when each actually happened, with overdue/in-progress status.
additional.showcase: Programme-management view of the ERM cycle.

additional.feature: Integration stubs (Oracle ERP, Salesforce CRM, Primavera P6)
additional.whatItDoes: Connection cards with live-alert/KPI tiles and sync metadata, ready to be wired to real systems.
additional.showcase: Shows the integration architecture is anticipated.

additional.whatItDoes: Pulls ABC's live ADX share price (with fallback source) onto the dashboard.
additional.showcase: A real, live external datapoint in the demo.

additional.whatItDoes: Every figure tagged Verified / Illustrative / Placeholder with click-through source attribution and an illustrative-data banner on each page.
additional.showcase: Builds trust — honest about what is real vs demo.

additional.whatItDoes: A single source of truth so every dashboard/brief/KPI shows identical headline numbers (no contradictions).
additional.showcase: Demo robustness — numbers always reconcile.

additional.whatItDoes: Links like ?focus=KRI-001 auto-scroll to and highlight the exact record across pages.
additional.showcase: Click a breached KRI to land on the exact data.

additional.whatItDoes: My-Dashboard and the approvals queue filter risks/actions by persona (Group CRO sees all; Champion sees own; Subsidiary CEO sees entity scope).
additional.showcase: A taste of role-based experience ahead of full RBAC.

additional.whatItDoes: One control swaps the demo client name (ABC to Aldar) live and hides the real name in print for confidentiality.
additional.showcase: Re-skin the demo for any prospect instantly.

additional.whatItDoes: Animated landing with theme switcher, live Gulf-time clock and guided navigation to core surfaces.
